description: Bird hopped in and out of the understory along the forest edge.  Behavior was very skittish never stopping in any spot for a long time (10-15 seconds at most).  No calls were given.  General size was hard to compare without other birds nearby but was simiar to other warblers I have seen.  Plummage seemed relatively intact - no molting appeared to be occurring.  Head, nape, face, back, and wings were all a similar shade of olive brown.  Eye was dark with a thin white eye-ring that was complete.  Throat was a bright yellow.  The edges of the nape came down on the dividing line between the throat and breast giving the impression of an incomplete olive-brown hood.  The breast of bird was also yellow and extended into the belly before showing faded yellow/whitish coloring on the lower belly and vent.  The head coloring did not contrast at all with the coloring of the back.  No differences in color amongst the different wing feather groups.  Could not determine the leg or beak color given the light conditions - both appeared grayish.
